goal: Remove the permanent test for deleted criterion-append syntax.
description: Scope: Closure: module - current edit-node CLI test cleanup; tests/test_manifest_tool_cli.py. Context: post-final static audit found one permanent negative test naming a deleted criterion-append interface. Target: retain repeated --criterion replacement and atomic invalidation coverage while removing the deleted syntax gate, then verify obsolete vocabulary once in this Node only. Design Considerations: current replacement behavior is covered by adjacent tests; an ephemeral text scan is sufficient migration evidence. Design Value: the active suite describes only the current edit-node contract. Constraints & Risks: no parser alias, compatibility path, production change, historical Plan rewrite, absolute path, full regression, or persisted runtime output.

interfaces.name: current_edit_node_criterion_test_contract
errors: atomic rejection of malformed current edits

dependencies.reason: durable tests cover only the current replacement interface

decisions.composition: delete one obsolete negative test and retain adjacent current tests
decisions.algorithms: no production algorithm change; one ephemeral linear text scan
decisions.state: completed Plan history remains immutable; active tests retain no deleted syntax
decisions.isolation: one test file is owned and no production file changes
decisions.concurrency: single-symbol deletion with no parallel writer

acceptance_criteria.text: Current edit-node CLI tests cover complete criterion replacement and atomic proof invalidation without a permanent deleted-syntax gate.
A one-time active-surface scan confirms the deleted criterion-append syntax name is absent from current documentation, source, and tests.

commit.message: test: remove obsolete criterion syntax gate

commands: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; name=\"--add-\"+\"criterion\"; roots=[Path(\"README.md\"),Path(\"SKILL.md\"),Path(\"references\"),Path(\"scripts\"),Path(\"tests\")]; files=[]; [files.append(p) for root in roots for p in ([root] if root.is_file() else root.rglob(\"*\")) if p.is_file() and p.suffix in {\".md\",\".py\"}]; raise SystemExit(1 if any(name in p.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") for p in files) else 0)"

goal: Revalidate the current-only CLI surface on a fresh repository fingerprint.
description: Scope: Closure: scenario - final validation of the obsolete CLI-test cleanup; complete repository suite and current Plan metadata. Context: the implementation Node removes the final permanent deleted-syntax gate after earlier Plans reached terminal history. Target: record one full current receipt, validate source paths and REQ-001 labels, then complete only after a fresh audit. Design Considerations: do not repeat the one-time migration scan; bind all durable commands to one content fingerprint. Design Value: preserves historical receipts while proving the new active surface independently. Constraints & Risks: no implementation change, manual criterion check, self-approval, repeated full run, absolute path, external Hook mutation, or raw output persistence.

acceptance_criteria.text: The complete unit suite passes exactly once for the reviewed current repository fingerprint.
The Better Plan workspace validates current source paths and REQ-001 labels while durable CLI tests cover only complete criterion replacement and atomic proof invalidation.
